1.dv: probably from G939 (βάσις) (through the notion of a foundation of power);

1.gl: to procreate (properly, of the father, but by extension of the mother); figuratively, to regenerate
1.kj: bear, beget, be born, bring forth, conceive, be delivered of, gender, make, spring

2.dv: probably from G939 (βάσις) (through the notion of a foundation of power);

2.gl: to procreate (properly, of the father, but by extension of the mother); figuratively, to regenerate
2.kj: bear, beget, be born, bring forth, conceive, be delivered of, gender, make, spring

3.dv: probably from G939 (βάσις) (through the notion of a foundation of power);

4.gl: to procreate (properly, of the father, but by extension of the mother); figuratively, to regenerate
4.kj: bear, beget, be born, bring forth, conceive, be delivered of, gender, make, spring

5.gl: a foreteller ("prophet"); by analogy, an inspired speaker; by extension, a poet
5.dv: from a compound of G4253 (πρό) and G5346 (φημί);

8.gl: something said (including the thought); by implication, a topic (subject of discourse), also reasoning (the mental faculty) or motive; by extension, a computation; specially, (with the article in John) the Divine Expres
8.kj: account, cause, communication, X concerning, doctrine, fame, X have to do, intent, matter, mouth, preaching, question, reason, + reckon, remove, say(-ing), shew, X speaker, speech,

9.dv: probably from G939 (βάσις) (through the notion of a foundation of power);

12.gl: figuratively, a magistrate; by Hebraism, very
12.dv: of uncertain affinity; a deity, especially (with G3588 (ὁ)) the supreme Divinity;

13.gl: supreme in authority, i.e. (as noun) controller; by implication, Master (as a respectful title)
compare G34 (ἀγέλη)) (to bring tidings); a messenger; especially an "angel"; by implication, a pastor
